violations.text: No allergen statement on menu. English Food Allergies What You Need to Know New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Food contact surfaces of ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back of the establishment, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Rice scoop observed stored with the handle in the rice. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Cleaned equipment and utensils are stored directly beside the mop sink. Equipment and utensils must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Utensils on the clean storage rack are displayed with the food contact surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
The plastic lid on the cold hold was not in good repair, with cracked corners and pieces of plastic missing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Aluminum wrap observed lining equipment. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
The counter top was not in good repair, with worn and shredded tape. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Ceramic kitchenware observed not in good repair, with chips in small dishes and bowls, and cracked plastic on utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
The back basement area has an accumulation of mud and pebbles. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment (dishwasher) or items were observed in the 3 bay sink area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed chicken prepared in advance at 45 degrees in a covered plastic container greater than 4 inches deep, with condensation of cover. PIC discussed with staff and disposed food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
Staff were observed emptying water bottle and rinsing it in the prep sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

An employee was observed rinsing their hands at the prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
An employee was observed drying their hands with their apron in the kitchen. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Rinsed lemons were observed in the hand-washing sink, a pot was observed in the hand-washing sink in the dish room. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the dish wash area or the beverage station. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC replaced bottle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
Staff were observed wiping a cutting board (on which raw chicken was placed) with a dry cloth. Staff informed to use sanitizing solution. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of bio film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Machine taken out of service, ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
Prohibited pest control chemicals (Evolve Mouse) was observed in basement storage room. PIC informed to cease use and remove. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked eggs and vegetables were placed hot in the prep units. Foods were moved to refrigeration to facilitate cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Staff were observed placing cooling eggs and vegetables into deep, covered plastic containers. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025

Working containers of dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings and a bag of restricted rodent control. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine and the quaternary ammonium solution for the 3-bay sink. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/09/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units (gaskets and under lids) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Alleyway where refuse, returnables, and recycables are located was observed with trash, litter and soil residue on ground. Area must be maintained to prevent harborage conditions for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
Establishment is using a sous vide cooking process for beef, pork, and eggs. Establishment must submit a HACCP Plan for all products. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/25/2024

Alleyway where refuse, returnables, and recycables are located was observed with trash, litter and soil residue on ground. Area must be maintained to prevent harborage conditions for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Raw animal foods (such as beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables) in the 2 door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection. Equipment must be cleaned and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment must use 3 bay sink to sanitize all dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Vacuum package pork and beef and broths were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Insecticide was observed on paper in basement. PIC informed and removed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Establishment is vacuum packaging and using a sous vide cooking process for beef and pork. Establishment must submit a HACCP plan for both products. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Various food boxes were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Knives and utensils were observed stored on the side of prep sink. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024

Alleyway where refuse, returnables and recyclables are located was observed with trash, litter and soil residue on ground. Lids for disposal units were also observed open. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Ice bin is lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
Dump sink at cookline was observed leaking. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
